summary: Iteration 3 testing complete. All 4 new feature groups (free-tier quota gating, subscription cancel, PDF CV upload, Remotive ingest) verified end-to-end. Backend: 19/19 pytest passes (quota, billing/cancel, /me/usage, PDF upload incl. validation, Remotive ingest+dedupe+source persistence, regression). Frontend: all 9 critical flows pass after a P0 fix to /app/frontend/src/pages/Jobs.jsx. UsageBanner appears+dismisses, /pricing CTA works, quota-error panel renders on JobDetail with Upgrade link, Billing cancel modal flow downgrades pro->free visibly, Profile Upload PDF picks file -> calls /api/profile/upload-cv -> populates headline+skills+target_roles, Jobs 'Pull fresh jobs' calls /api/jobs/ingest, Remotive 'live · remotive' badge renders on cards.

ui_bugs.issue: P0 build break: file had two issues — (1) used <UsageBanner /> without an import statement, (2) trailing garbage characters '1717A";\n}' at line 145 caused a Babel parse error. Frontend was returning a webpack overlay / error and the entire Jobs route was unrenderable until fixed. I patched the file (added the import + removed the stray characters) and verified the page now renders.
ui_bugs.priority: CRITICAL (already fixed by me, please review)

action_items: Review the Jobs.jsx fix I applied — the trailing garbage strongly suggests a previous failed edit/merge. Please double-check the file is what you intended (especially the scoreColor helper at the bottom).

Carryover from iter 2 (NOT a regression this iteration): GET /api/billing/status/{session_id} can still 500 if Stripe metadata isn't a plain dict — main agent already aware. /api/billing/me is the safe path for the frontend to read plan state, which the new /billing page correctly uses.
critical_code_review_comments: quota.py: clean and correct. usage_summary returns matches_limit=null for paid plans — frontend banner correctly skips rendering. Auto-downgrade-on-expiry is in get_effective_plan, good.

routes_jobs.py compute_match: cache check happens BEFORE quota check, so re-running an already-computed match never costs a quota slot (verified). 6th distinct match returns 403 with detail.code='quota_exceeded' — matches spec exactly.

routes_billing.py /cancel: correctly sets cancelled_at, previous_plan; clears plan_expires_at. Idempotent on free plan. No Stripe sub to cancel (one-time charge model) — message text is honest about it.

routes_profile.py upload-cv: validates ext, size (5MB), emptiness; rejects image-only PDFs via a 100-char minimum extracted-text guard — good UX guard. Stores cv_filename + cv_bytes alongside cv_text.
Jobs.jsx had a P0 syntax bug + missing import — likely a stale edit was never cleaned up. Suggest adding an ESLint pre-commit or running lint in CI to catch these.
UsageBanner: dismiss state is local (useState) — banner reappears on next page nav. That's likely intentional but worth confirming with PM; could store to sessionStorage if undesired.
Frontend api.js uses withCredentials presumably — cookie auth worked end-to-end. No CORS errors observed.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/pages/Jobs.jsx (added UsageBanner import, removed trailing parse-error garbage)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 9 iter3 flows + Jobs page after fix)

test_credentials: Bearer test_session_career_os / cookie session_token=test_session_career_os (user_testseed01). Pre-existing in DB. PDF generated on the fly with reportlab.
seed_data_creation: During testing I (a) reset match_usage to count=5 for the current month to force banner to render, (b) flipped user.plan between free/pro to exercise cancel + unlimited-pro paths, (c) ran /api/jobs/ingest a few times so ~25 real Remotive jobs are now in the jobs collection. Final state: user reset to plan='free', match_usage cleared, decisions cleared.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 3 (quota gating, billing cancel, PDF upload, Remotive ingest) is fully working. Watch out for: Jobs.jsx had a stale parse error that I fixed — verify Jobs.jsx tail (the scoreColor helper) looks clean before any future edit. The DB now contains ~25 real Remotive-sourced jobs; if test counts feel off, dedupe is the reason. /api/billing/status 500 from iter 2 is still open (third-party lib issue, not iter 3 work).
rca_of_the_issue: Jobs.jsx P0 parse error: at line 145 the file contained the string '1717A";' followed by an extra '}' — appears to be a botched search/replace where the last 4 chars of '#71717A' were duplicated. Babel raised 'Identifier directly after number (145:4)' and webpack returned a compile error overlay, making the entire /jobs route fail. Compounding bug: <UsageBanner /> was referenced in JSX but never imported. Both fixed in a single edit pass; webpack now compiles successfully (verified in /var/log/supervisor/frontend.out.log: 'Compiled successfully!') and the Jobs page renders with banner, ingest button, remotive badges, and job grid all working.
